Executive Summary:
Business Launch Formula Example from my Initial Business
• 	Initially I began teaching people about the stock market (without any credentials)
and started a newsletter that went out to 19 people. That 19-person list eventually
grew into 80,000 people - how does this happen?
• 	Focus on getting people to opt in - this should be the number one metric you operate
by. All along you must continue to test and tweak the process to increase your opt in
conversation rate
• 	The initial list was made up of people who were interested in investing. Around the
time it reached 1,600 people, a mentor offered a suggestion to partner together and
create a product for traders
• 	So we created a launch list for this by offering content on trading to the investor
newsletter (although didn’t call it that at the time). That first launch ended up doing
$1,650
• 	We continued doing launches after this and got better each time. We did around 2-3
launches per year. Later on I discovered a key group on my list and called them the
“middle ground” - people that traded more often than investors but less than the
traders
• 	I created a launch for this from the “investor” and “trader” list to a new launch list.
This launch did $106,000, which was the first one over 6- figures. We had very
limited costs for this launch, and had JV’s
• 	All it took was responding the market demand…
• 	Next, I came up with an idea to perform webinars - I realized people just wanted to
learn directly. Each list got fed into the webinar list and they were sold to from there.
The webinars were doing around $30,000
• 	From this webinar I created a Home Study Course - this again drove everyone into
this new course. Each time it was simply a matter of responding to what the market
wanted
• 	If you do this, it will lead to creating a portfolio of products and building a
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

relationship with your list. And as you know, this is what it’s all about in the end
Key Takeaways:
• 	Your focus should be on getting people to opt in
• 	Listen closely to your list and find out exactly what it is they want
• 	Create a portfolio of products and a successful business by building a relationship
with your list

TRANSCRIPT:
This is next example is actually my example, back when I started
my first online business.
This is where I was teaching people about the stock market. I had no
credentials what so ever. I wasn’t a stock broker, registered investment advisor or
anything like that. I was just someone who had a passion for the stock market and
spent a lot of time studying it.
I started a newsletter about the stock market. That first newsletter went out to
about 19 people. I mentioned that to you earlier. It was actually 17 people. It was 19
email addresses. One was my second email address and one was my wife’s email
address, so it was 19.
Here’s that list. This is just like John started, except he had that 1,800 people.
I had 19 people. That list that started there with 19 eventually grew into 80,000
people. It’s sort of ironic. That’s the number John’s at right now. I’m sure he’s going
bigger, but I got to about 80,000.
How do you go from 19 people to 80,000 people? Very simple. I really
focused on getting people to opt in. That was like the number one metric in my sight
was how many people I got to opt in every day. I had a graph and I graphed every
single day how many people I got to opt in.
It started off with every few days I would get one. Then it got up to the point
where I would get one every day, then three or four every day. Eventually I was
getting 30 to 35 people per day. That’s like 1,000 people a month. That’s 12,000
people a year. I did that for seven or eight years. Do the math and you end up with
around 80,000.
Of course you get some unsubscribes along the way, but every now and then I
would do some promotions and stuff. I ended up just over 80,000 people. All along I
kept on testing and tweaking the process, working on getting people to opt in.
In any case, this is this list. The thing about this list was (I’m going to do a
little modification right here) it wasn’t just a list it was a list of investors. I don’t know
if they were investors. They were people interested in investing. They were interested
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Two – Business Launch Formula – Part 3
5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
in the stock market from an investing stand point.
For those of you who don’t know much about the stock market, there’s
investors and there’s also traders. Traders are interested in the market as well, but
investors usually have a longer time frame. Investors that are in and out of the
market. Investors go in the market and stay in the market.
There’s this other universe. It’s similar, both interested in the stock market,
but these folks are more intensely interested and more short-term, in and out of the
market. I had an interest in both of these areas. I had knowledge about both of these
areas. I started this list, and after I got that list, I think it was around 1,600 people
after four, five or six months.
At that point I actually had someone who was a mentor. That mentor was a
trader, and he said, “Hey Jeff, you’ve got some success here. You’re building up some
success with publishing.”
I’ve got to mention, first of all these are free. This is a free newsletter. I wasn’t
charging anything; I was just building this list hoping someday I would make some
money from it really.
My mentor said to me, “Let’s partner up and create a product for traders.”
That’s what we did. This was that first launch that did $1650. What I did was I
created a little mini launch. This was that very first launch.
What I did was I created a launch list. I didn’t call it a launch list at the time. I
didn’t know what a launch list was, no one did frankly. I created a second list. I
invited people to join this list, gave them a bunch of free content for about a month,
then I made this off for this product.
We did this $1650 and we were off to the races and kept on building from
there. We did launch after launch after launch. That’s where I did the $6000, then we
did the $8000 and we kept on getting better. Eventually we did a $17,000 launch,
but we kept on getting better, doing about two to three launches a year.
Basically, I tool this investing list. Then I rolled off a launch list and then I
sold people into this trading list. We kept on doing these occasional launches. We
kept this for sale every day of the year, but two to three times a year we put together a
sale, a promotion.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
We would offer a discount. We would offer a bonus with an extra report, extra
subscription time and we would create a launch, and do a promotion for that and do
another launch.
Then I’m thought, “Okay, well this is going along pretty good.” Then I found
the middle ground. What I learned in publishing to both of these groups is that there
was this middle group. They were people that traded more often than the investors,
but they traded less often than the full-out traders. These are sort of long-term
traders or short- term investors so to speak.
I found out that this big group was out there. I found that out because these
people would always write back to me what they wanted. What we did was we created
another launch list. We created this product, created another launch list. Didn’t
create a product, actually created a launch for it, sent people from the investors list
and sent people from the traders list into this launch list.
It’s a little more complicated because actually this traders list, we had a
prospect list and a buyers list, so we sent all three of those universes into the launch
list for this one, and then we launched this thing.
That launch was that $106,000 launch. That was the first launch I did that
did over six figures. That was a great launch, beautiful launch, it was incredible. It
was all profit. We had no partners. We had no costs. We hadn’t created the product
yet. The product was actually - they were going to get a daily email from us telling
them what we thought was going to happen the next day in the stock market.
Literally there was no cost. We were going to send them for a year’s
subscription one email a day, so we were going to send them 250 short emails that
said, “We think the market is going up, or it’s going down.” Literally, no costs to
fulfill. There’s no JVs, nothing, just $106,000. After merchant expenses we ended up
with $102,000 or $103,000.
That was completely awesome. The thing is we just responded to the market
demand. These people said, “Hey, we know that you’re doing this short-term trading
stuff, but I’m not a short-term trader. I’m a day trader. How can I use all of your
wisdom?”
These folks said, “Well, you know that day trading stuff is really cool, but I’ve
got a job. I can’t do that. I need something a little longer term. I’d like to trade on a
daily basis.” We just responded to that market demand, made up this offer, put
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

people on a launch list and ran them through the sequence. Now we’re starting to
talk about some real money. Right?
You know what? It didn’t end there, of course it doesn’t. Just like John
Gallagher, you’re going to come up with more ideas. So then we came up with this
idea for webinars. We did these paid webinars. We would sit there and watch the
market, and make comments throughout the day. People would pay us $200 to $300
to watch our comments for a day a two, just to learn from us.
What did we do then? We created another launch list. We just fed people
from each one of these into this launch list and then we sold the webinar. It didn’t
end there. I think we were doing, let’s just say, $30,000. It was at least $30,000. It
might have been more. We would do these a few times a year.
Then there was the home study course. What did we do? We did another one.
We just drove everyone into the home study course. We rolled that out. The first time
we rolled that out, that thing did $110,000. Again, that was no partners, no affiliates.
We did have some costs which were shipping out books and DVDs and CDs.
Each one of these, we just responded to what the market was telling us. We
were building up a product portfolio. That’s the Business Launch Formula. You’re
doing these launches. You’re creating great products. You’re doing great products
because you’re doing what the market is asking you for. You’re doing it in a very
interactive fashion. You’re building list relationship all the way along. You’re just
doing what the market tells you, and next thing you know you turn around and
you’ve got a whole bunch of products and you’re on a roll.
